Addressing the lack of killer apps, CZ noted that 15 years of heavy government suppression killed many apps before they could grow. He predicts micro-payments for future AI agents will absolutely rely on blockchain, either via Bitcoin L2s or centralized enterprise chains. — link

text: Fed Proposes "Skinny Master Accounts" for Crypto and Fintech Firms According to crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett, the Federal Reserve has proposed a "skinny master account" framework that could allow eligible crypto and fintech firms to access the Fed's payment system for clearing and settlement. The Fed also asked regional Reserve Banks to pause decisions on new Tier 3 account applications through December 2026 to promote consistency before the final framework is implemented. Most crypto firms are classified as Tier 3 institutions. — link

text: NVIDIA Q1 Revenue Rises 85% to $81.6 Billion, Guides $91 Billion for Next Quarter NVIDIA reported fiscal Q1 2027 revenue of $81.6 billion, up 85% year-over-year and 20% sequentially, while Data Center revenue increased 92% to $75.2 billion. Non-GAAP diluted EPS rose 140% to $1.87, and non-GAAP gross margin was 75.0%. For fiscal Q2 2027, NVIDIA expects revenue of approximately $91 billion, plus or minus 2%, and said its outlook does not assume any Data Center compute revenue from China. Operating cash flow reached $50.3 billion during the quarter, while free cash flow totaled $48.6 billion. — link

text: FOMC Minutes: Most Officials See Longer Path Back to 2% Inflation, Further Tightening Possible if Needed The Federal Reserve's April FOMC meeting minutes showed officials broadly viewed inflation as still running above the 2% target, with higher energy prices, the Middle East conflict, tariffs, and AI-related investment costs contributing to upside inflation risks. Most officials said inflation could take longer than previously expected to return to target levels. Most participants also indicated that additional policy tightening could become necessary if inflation remains persistently above target. Officials added that rate cuts could become appropriate if inflation eases further or labor market conditions weaken materially. — link

text: 𝐚𝐧𝐠𝐞𝐥 𝐧𝐮𝐦𝐛𝐞𝐫𝐬 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐧𝐠𝐞 𝟖𝟖𝟖𝟖𝟖𝟖 𝐭𝐨 𝟖𝟖𝟖𝟖𝟖𝟖𝟖 swapped 560000 $POD for 163504 $USDC price at time of selling: 0.305 $ PnL: +60514$ (+57.75%) was yesterday. had the urge to sell into volume spike but refrained. fell into the believer hole again. this whole trade was fucked up. first, i held too long and suffered the whole time because i have low tolerance to volatility. i sold later (and lower) and been going through hell ever since. i am fighting demons. strong fomo. trying to rationalize and find reasons for re-entering all day. the same old problem. i have extensive data that proves that its -EV for me to re-enter trades shortly after closing. i simulated what my NW would look like if i had exclusively traded first run-up and it is clear what i have to do. trade first run-up and ignore the asset in perpetuity ever after (that is an individual thing, i know people who trade in and out the same assets hundreds of times and it works for them but that is not me). yet, i struggle hard with that. and trading in public amplifies this, because it is set in stone that as soon as price is moving 0.2% above my selling price some smooth brain writes a comment that reads "hahahaha y22 has sold the stone bottom. what a retard." the second thing that makes it hard is social influence in group chats, the feeling when smth is mooning and everybody is seemingly in and euphoria posting and oneself is sidelined feels bad. ofc we love to see the homies winning and its not about being jealous. it is about a feeling of self-worth. seeing all these PnL cards creates the illusion that everybody is winning but me. many of us tie their feeling of self-worth to the number on the screen and we feel bad when we think we are underperforming compared to everybody else. that stuff is what keeps us trapped. i have a long history falling for this trap. some here maybe remember how i went full shizo and opened and closed launchcoin positions every waking hour for days. happened with Asteroid too lately, although on smaller scale. so, what do i do? how to combat this? i remind myself to stick to my process and that refraining from re-entering is +EV for me in the long run, regardless if the asset i just sold goes up or down. i have extensive data from my trade history that backs that up, and if smth goes higher after i sell then i have to accept that and force myself to also see the other side of this, namely the stuff i TPed and that went to the gulag after and never recovered. i will not have huge multiples but i will also not have roundtrips, that is the contract i signed. i am a swing trader with a good track record in very specific constellations, and that is okay. gotta accept that and stay in my lane. second, i delete the asset from my watchlist. i have not checked $POD price since i have sold yesterday. that was crazy hard for me, the urge is extremely strong. i crashed out a dozen times today already and tried to convince myself to rebuy. the battle is still ongoing, will keep u posted how this situation progresses. thirdly, i ignore all line of thoughts that start with "what if" and "maybe". straight up ignoring, i don't engage in mapping out hypothetic scenarios in my head about assets i don't own anymore. swapped 50000 $USDC for 92608111 $BNKR mc at time of buying: 50m $ 𝐡𝐨𝐥𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬: 933504 USDC 92m BNKR 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐛𝐚𝐫 (𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐳𝐞𝐝): ▮▮▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯ 10.50%

text: DeFi Derivatives Protocol Variational Raises $50 Million Series A Led by Dragonfly Decentralized derivatives trading protocol Variational has raised $50 million in a Series A round led by Dragonfly Capital, with participation from Bain Capital Crypto and Coinbase Ventures. The project aims to aggregate liquidity from major crypto exchanges and traditional financial market makers through a blockchain-based platform, supporting trading of real-world assets such as oil and commodities. Bain Capital Crypto previously led Variational's $10.3 million seed round in 2021. — link

text: Copper Seeks $500 Million Sale as Crypto IPO Market Stalls U.K.-based crypto custody firm Copper is exploring a sale at an estimated $500 million valuation. The company had previously considered an IPO, but the crypto listing market has remained subdued amid weaker bitcoin prices and continued capital flows into AI. Copper's main business is ClearLoop, an in-custody settlement platform designed for institutional trading. — link

text: The Bank of England plans to publish draft rules for systemic stablecoins next month and finalize them by year-end, while it may set temporary limits on total stablecoin issuance to mitigate early risks. text: White House Signals Imminent Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Update Patrick Witt, executive director of the White House President's Council of Advisors for Digital Assets, said a formal update on the U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve is nearing release, citing a "breakthrough" on the legal basis, custody arrangements, and an interagency reporting framework. The reserve was established by President Trump's March 6, 2025 executive order, which directs Treasury to custody forfeited BTC in the reserve and prohibits selling BTC held there. — link

text: Verus–Ethereum Bridge Exploited for Over $11M A cross-chain bridge between Verus and Ethereum was attacked for more than $11 million. Blockaid said the issue resembled past bridge exploits like Wormhole and Nomad, stemming from verification that failed to ensure source-chain parameters matched Ethereum-side payment data, and could be fixed with roughly 10 lines of code. Some Verus block-producing nodes went offline after the incident, briefly halting block production. — link

text: USDT Gains $5B as Other Stablecoins Shrink Total stablecoin supply has topped $300 billion, but growth has sharply slowed. Over the past month, Tether's USDT expanded by more than $5 billion, while USDC, USDe and PYUSD together fell by about $4.2 billion—leaving net stablecoin growth at only around $0.9 billion, or roughly 0.3% for the month. The report said new inflows are concentrating in USDT rather than expanding the overall market. Ethena's USDe supply dropped 28% over the month and is down nearly 34% year-to-date, attributed to compressed perp funding rates reducing yields; Sky's USDS and World Liberty Financial's USD1 picked up some inflows, while PYUSD fell 13% over the month. — link

text: Highlight Clip Vitalik Buterin: L2s afraid to fully decentralize should just be centralized servers On July 3, 2025, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin stated in an EthCC speech video that many Layer 2 projects claiming to build on-chain retain instant backdoors or fall short of their claimed decentralization stages. He believes that from a practical standpoint, this architecture is not much better than a traditional centralized server. Vitalik Buterin stated that if developers are afraid to make their projects truly decentralized, the most straightforward approach is to simply build a centralized server. — link

text: Trump-Linked Truth Social Withdraws Spot Bitcoin ETF Filing Bloomberg ETF analyst James Seyffart noted that Truth Social has withdrawn its spot Bitcoin ETF filing. He suggested the decision may reflect intensifying competition in spot Bitcoin ETFs, especially after Morgan Stanley's MSBT entered with a 14-basis-point fee. Seyffart added that Truth Social may instead pivot toward a more flexible crypto ETF structure under the Investment Company Act of 1940. — link
